task_statement: P5c narrow implementation Building: close declared_gate_refs absence and invalid transition_concern pending-target leakage using prior P5a/P5b design evidence, through the official brick build graph route.
selection_rule: manual narrow graph after P5a/P5b multi-adapter design attempts collected design evidence but stalled before implementation. This reroute limits the next Building to implementation + code attack QA + closure to avoid repeating broad design fan-out while preserving official brick build route.

nodes.work_statement:
Implement the P5 narrow fix from prior design evidence.

Observed current seams to close:
1. support/operator/plan_validation.py::_validate_declared_gate_refs_for_link_row currently accepts missing declared_gate_refs on active Link rows. Change active validation so a Link row missing declared_gate_refs is RED. Preserve existing rules for explicit refs: non-empty, no duplicates, first ref link-gate:default-transition, all refs admitted. Do not add a new gate or Movement literal. If a materializer wants default-transition, it must stamp explicit declared_gate_refs before runtime.
2. support/operator/walker_transition_concern.py::_build_invalid_transition_concern_hold currently derives target_brick from raw invalid concern refs before falling back to source. Change invalid concern HOLD construction so invalid transition_concern_evidence always HOLDs at source_brick_ref and does not copy raw proposed refs into immediate_target_ref / target_brick / pending_target_ref.
3. Add focused negative regression coverage in support/checkers/check_bounded_agent_proposed_routing_loop0.py: malformed transition_concern_evidence containing a resolvable related_boundary_refs target must pause with HOLD target fields equal to the source brick, not the proposed target. Also add/adjust a validation probe for active post-expansion Link row missing declared_gate_refs RED, with explicit link-gate:default-transition GREEN.
4. Preserve positive behavior: valid concern/adopted declared route behavior still passes; HOLD is lifecycle not Link Movement.

Run and return exact results for: py_compile changed Python, focused bounded_agent_proposed_routing_loop profile, link_routing_behavioral profile if touched/needed, git diff --check, and check_profile.py --all. Return changed_files and not_proven. If --all fails out of scope, classify honestly.

STRICT SCOPE: phase:P5 narrow implementation only. Use prior P5a/P5b design outputs as support evidence, not source truth. Do not broaden into P6-P9, release/dashboard/provider/pytest honesty, raw scrub, post-HOLD resume isolation, P3 return-shape, or P4 AgentFact intake except as cited support evidence. Do not push. Do not use ai-cli MCP, ai-cli-backed helpers, or native subagents. Do not store or print secrets/session bodies. Public/operator language is build()/brick build only. Movement vocabulary is forward/reroute; HOLD/pause is lifecycle. QA may inspect and run probe_write fixtures only; do not source-mutate in QA. Checker/profile green is support evidence only.

Code attack QA for P5c. Inspect the diff and run focused checks. Required attacks: missing declared_gate_refs on active Link row is rejected; explicit default-transition row remains accepted; invalid malformed transition_concern_evidence with one resolvable proposed target cannot set immediate_target_ref/target_brick/pending_target_ref to that proposed target; valid/adopted concern path remains intact. Confirm changed files are inside declared scope and no support surface gained Movement, success, quality, source truth, or hidden target-selector authority. Return local non-binding transition_concern_evidence targeting work if implementation_gap or boundary_mismatch remains.

STRICT SCOPE: phase:P5 narrow implementation only. Use prior P5a/P5b design outputs as support evidence, not source truth. Do not broaden into P6-P9, release/dashboard/provider/pytest honesty, raw scrub, post-HOLD resume isolation, P3 return-shape, or P4 AgentFact intake except as cited support evidence. Do not push. Do not use ai-cli MCP, ai-cli-backed helpers, or native subagents. Do not store or print secrets/session bodies. Public/operator language is build()/brick build only. Movement vocabulary is forward/reroute; HOLD/pause is lifecycle. QA may inspect and run probe_write fixtures only; do not source-mutate in QA. Checker/profile green is support evidence only.

Final P5c closure. Fan in work and code-QA bodies. Report observed_evidence / narrowly_proven / not_proven / remaining_delta / parent_goal_delta_status / next_target_candidates. P5 done condition for this narrow slice: missing declared_gate_refs RED or explicit default materialization only; invalid concern target leakage closed; focused profile(s), py_compile, git diff --check, and check_profile.py --all are green or gaps are clearly not_proven; changed files match scope. If any implementation/boundary/evidence gap remains, return Link-facing transition_concern_evidence targeting work. Do not claim whole goal/customer-ready complete.

STRICT SCOPE: phase:P5 narrow implementation only. Use prior P5a/P5b design outputs as support evidence, not source truth. Do not broaden into P6-P9, release/dashboard/provider/pytest honesty, raw scrub, post-HOLD resume isolation, P3 return-shape, or P4 AgentFact intake except as cited support evidence. Do not push. Do not use ai-cli MCP, ai-cli-backed helpers, or native subagents. Do not store or print secrets/session bodies. Public/operator language is build()/brick build only. Movement vocabulary is forward/reroute; HOLD/pause is lifecycle. QA may inspect and run probe_write fixtures only; do not source-mutate in QA. Checker/profile green is support evidence only.

proof_limits: official build evidence is support evidence only
P5c closure does not prove P6-P9 or customer-ready whole goal
single-adapter narrow reroute reuses prior multi-adapter design evidence but does not prove Gemini/Claude QA for implementation
checker green is support evidence only
not_proven: semantic completeness of all future transition concern or portfolio adoption patterns
